item.description:
Data governance can be tedious, overbearing and complicated — especially for small teams who just want quick, reliable answers from their data.

LLMs are powerful but hallucinate. For governance purposes, that’s a dealbreaker. So what if you grounded it strictly in your own metadata — no guessing, no fabrication, just your data answering for itself?

A weekend project to implement something like it? Open source, lightweight, and something most data engineers could spin up without a vendor conversation.

As digital regulations like age verification reshape the internet, businesses are entering a new era of regulated data responsibility.

What was once simple user data is now evolving into high-risk identity data—biometrics, government IDs, and verified age signals. This shift demands more than just compliance checklists. It requires a fundamental rethink of data governance and engineering.

Businesses need to stay ahead by embedding governance directly into their data architecture.

Few points to consider while organisations are navigating this shift:

AI Agents are quietly reshaping what it means to be a Data Engineer.

The work is shifting — less time optimising SQL and orchestrating pipelines, more time designing scalable frameworks and governance structures for AI to operate within.

That sounds like an evolution. But here’s the uncomfortable part: the architectural judgment and domain knowledge that used to take years to develop? It’s increasingly a prompt away.

The Data Engineering world is going to feel this shift all at once — and most teams are still heads down in the day-to-day to see it coming.

item.title: Not so quaint life of Melbourne through the lens of a reluctant tourist
item.description: A wonderful weekend in Melbourne

California now requires all OS providers to collect age data just to run an operating system.

Australia’s Online Safety Act mandates age verification for social media and R-rated gaming.

The Digital ID push is accelerating across both countries.

Noble intent. Genuinely.

But every one of these policies has the same blind spot:

They legislate what data to collect. Not what happens to it after.

From a data governance and engineering perspective, this isn’t just a privacy concern — it’s a platform stress test that most organisations are not ready for.

item.title: The AI revolution has legs but the vision still needs a human mind
item.description: A mind that holds all the context and the memories

Here is a great initiative to try your luck at self hosting -

x.com/dhh/status/203358…

I self hosted Campfire and Writebook for internal chat and documentation on my own server. Seamless set up and even added a sub domain for overall access.

Just starting with Fizzy and liking it so far and will self host it as well.

One of the main reasons to self host some of these services is the availability of software like ONCE which makes it easy to set up and maintain.

🏆 We’re proud to share that Etherion Consulting LLP has been named Best Boutique Data-Driven Transformation Consultancy 2025 by Wealth & Finance International — Management Consulting Awards.

We received this recognition in November 2025, and we’ve been sitting with it ever since — because it means more to us than a trophy on a shelf.

item.title: Learning to Love a new City
item.description: The beautiful city of Sydney and the ways it is making me fall in love with it

item.title: The World I want to live is not in between Past and the Future
item.description: The World I want to live is not in between Past and the Future but its too late to go back and too soon to look ahead.

item.title: Seasons - They are a changin'
item.description: A beautiful evening in St. Leonards

item.title: TUI over GUI, and we might be onto something unprecedented
item.description: Terminal User Interface, as the name suggests, is a command-line utility with a user interface for navigation. Each application boasts a keyboard-driven user interface for increased productivity and easy navigation in the app.

item.title: AI is this generation's Kryptonite
item.description: Don't let the GPT-4, 5, or the mini models overtake your life. There is a sane middle ground with newer technology, as it has been the case for decades gone by.

item.title: The World of Arch is Fun with Omarchy
item.description: Relearning to build my own systems with my preferences to love technology again.

item.title: Amsterdam - The City of Canals
item.description: A time loop captured on Fuji non 18-55mm lens with FujiFilm X-E4

item.title: The Archivist on the Chain - Continued
item.description: The world is struggling for one group of people while others revel in the destruction for their own vanity. Something is about to change and it comes from the past.

item.title: The Archivist on the Chain
item.description: The world is struggling for one group of people while others revel in the destruction for their own vanity. Something is about to change and it comes from the past.

item.title: Barcelona - The Beautiful Coastal City in Catalonia
item.description: Barcelona: The Beautiful Coastal City of Catalonia, where the sunshine is bright and the food is delicious. From the Gothic Quarter in the city center of Barcelona to Park Güell and the Picasso Museum, the city celebrates history & tradition.

item.title: Budapest - A Charming City
item.description: Allow yourself to be immersed in its vibe.

item.title: The Perfect Future was in the Past
item.description: The world is a mess and it seems there is no way to fix it. Did we leave our perfect world behind?

item.title: The World We Live Is Devoid Of Nuances
item.description: When did we lose our ability to understand and balance our opinions in nuances?

item.title: Tariffs, Inflation & AI - Why the World Feels More Chaotic Than Ever
item.description: The leadership in the world has degraded drastically and so we have a choatic world where only common man suffers.

item.title: AI & Humans - How do we co-exist?
item.description: The world has become Technology first but is there a way for us to keep it Humans or Humanity first where Technology plays a supplementary role?

item.title: About
item.description:
Hey there! 👋🏼

I’m Vaibhav Kalekar. Principal Data Engineer with 15 years of experience building the data infrastructure that powers the digital world — the pipelines, the platforms, the governance frameworks that make modern technology run. I’ve done this across three continents in cities like: Mumbai, Chicago, Munich, and now Sydney.

I know how the machine works. That’s exactly why I write about what it’s doing to us.

My writing sits at an uncomfortable intersection — between the technical and the human, the analytical and the lyrical, the world as it is being built and the world as it feels to live in. I write essays about technology, AI, and the slow erosion of things worth keeping. I write fiction to say the things that essays can’t. And I photograph the cities and landscapes I move through, because some moments deserve to be held still.
